files.notes: This appears to be a misextracted frontispiece caption or photo credit. The PDF pages show article content about Mary Ann Frost Stearns Pratt and birth control discussions, but no standalone article by Lucy Burnham about absence or Kirtland, New Mexico. This is likely a header artifact or photo caption that was incorrectly treated as an article.

files.complete_text:
LESSON II.

Work and Business.

SECOND WEEK IN FEBRUARY.

THE DIGNITY OF LABOR.

References: Gen. 2:15; 3:19; Ex. 20:9; 31:1-11; Neh. 4:6; Prov. 6:6-11; 10:4, 5; 12:11, 24; 13:4; 14:23; 18:9; 20:4; 21:25; 22:29; 24:30-34; 27:23-27; 31:10-31; Eccl. 3:13; 5:12; 9:10; Matt. 20:1-16; Luke 10:7; John 5:17; Acts 18:3; 20:34, 35; I Cor. 4:12; Eph. 4:28; I Thess. 4:11; II Thess. 3:10-12; I Tim. 5:8.

Work is not a curse, but a blessing. It was instituted in Eden before the fall. "And the Lord God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it." (Gen. 2:15.) After the fall, work became toilsome, but it was not then first imposed. "In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread." (Gen. 3:19.) The curse was not work, but the sweat and toil that must accompany work in a world where thorns and thistles grow.

Work is honorable. No honest work is degrading. The man who works with his hands is as honorable as he who works with his brain. "Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings." (Prov. 22:29.) Paul was not ashamed to work with his hands. "Yea, ye yourselves know, that these hands have ministered unto my necessities, and to them that were with me." (Acts 20:34.) Jesus was a carpenter. (Mark 6:3.)

Work is necessary. "If any would not work, neither should he eat." (II Thess. 3:10.) This is God's law. Society cannot afford to support the idle. Every man should contribute his share to the world's work.

Work is beneficial. It develops character. It teaches patience, perseverance, self-reliance. It gives satisfaction and happiness. "The sleep of a laboring man is sweet." (Eccl. 5:12.) Idleness is the mother of vice.

Work should be done well. "Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might." (Eccl. 9:10.) Half-hearted work is poor work. The man who does his work well, whatever it may be, will succeed.

Work should be done honestly. "Let him labor, working with his hands the thing which is good." (Eph. 4:28.) Dishonest work is not work at all, but theft.

Work should be done cheerfully. "I have showed you all things, how that so laboring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive." (Acts 20:35.)

QUESTIONS.

When was work first instituted?
What was the curse pronounced after the fall?
Is any honest work degrading?
What does Paul say about working with the hands?
What was Christ's trade?
What is God's law about work and eating?
What are some of the benefits of work?
How should work be done?
What kind of work should we avoid?
What spirit should characterize our work?
files.title: Work and Business
